title: merremia gallabatensis
intro: Merremia gallabatensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae, commonly known as the morning glory family. It is a perennial climber native to Ethiopia, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves, stems, and roots. Traditionally, Merremia gallabatensis has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of Merremia gallabatensis often involve boiling the plant parts in water or making a paste with the leaves and roots for topical or internal use.
